articles.title: Elon Musk tweets his support over proposed Dogecoin changes

articles.title: Crypto Newcomer Backed by Coinbase Soars 50% in One Week, Defying Bitcoin and Altcoin Markets
articles.description: One small-cap crypto newcomer is defying the widespread downturn that’s hammered Bitcoin and the altcoin markets at large. articles.title: Filecoin Now Has Its Own Stablecoin. But Why?
articles.description: ICHI, a protocol for creating “decentralized money authorities,” has launched a new stablecoin for the Filecoin network called "oneFIL."

articles.title: Polygon (MATIC) Announces Launch Of New Blockchain Project, ‘Avail’
articles.description: The press releases are on fire today as Polygon has announcements firing out left and right. The platform has announced ‘Avail’ – described as “a robust general-purpose scalable data availability layer”. Additionally, the platform announced the launch of yield aggregator Pickle.Finance. articles.title: Mexican billionaire says he’ll hold Bitcoin for 30 years. He’s also bearish on Dogecoin

articles.title: Hackers are Using Infected PC Games to Make Millions Mining Crypto
articles.description: Antivirus company Avast has discovered a new malware named “Crackonosh” that is being hidden in free versions of popular PC games. The malware hijacks your computer to be used in crypto mining.

articles.title: Ethereum Founder Burns Billions Worth Of Coins
articles.description: Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin announced that he has destroyed trillions of SHIB coins. He burned approximately 420 trillion worth of SHIB coins. This translates to about $6 billion worth of coins. According to Buterin, this was about 90% of his entire portfolio of SHIB coins. The creator of SHIB sent the coins to Buterin in […]

articles.title: Internet Computer (ICP) logs revenge recovery after crashing 95% since launch
articles.description: The long-awaited rebound came as the community accused the Internet Computer team of dumping $6 billion worth of ICP tokens.

articles.title: SEC Moves To Block Ripple From Questioning Former SEC Director William Hinman
articles.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is attempting to block Ripple from calling upon the SEC’s former director of division of corporate finance to testify in the ongoing lawsuit between the two entities. According to a new court filing, the SEC requests that William Hinman not be deposed, claiming that Ripple does not have […] The post SEC Moves To Block Ripple From Questioning Former SEC Director William Hinman appeared first on The Daily Hodl.
